messages.content: Great! What is the topic of your quiz?

You are hired by the health department to conduct a needs assessment of people with diabetes. The PRECEDE model provides a logical series of steps to describe all the factors that influence diabetes in the community. You complete a series of phased assessments. In the behavioral assessment you should examine:
Group of answer choices

environmental links to the behavior, or outcomes of interest.

Predisposing, reinforcing, and enabling factors that influence behaviors

behavioral links to the problems identified in the social or epidemiological analysis

alignment of an intervention to existing policy and administration

The correct answer is:

Predisposing, reinforcing, and enabling factors that influence behaviors

In your assessment of behaviors related to diabetes you determine a complex array of behaviors that influence the occurrence of diabetes symptoms including monitoring and adjusting diet, physical activity, insulin administration, and health care visits. How would you BEST categorize this total set of behaviors?
Group of answer choices

Health-promoting behavior

Screening and early detection behavior

Adherence behavior

Self-management behavior

The BEST categorization for the total set of behaviors related to diabetes would be:

Self-management behavior

You are developing a PRECEDE model on cardiovascular health. It is important that your limited budget be directed to interventions that target the highest priority behaviors. Which of the following statements is not one of the steps to prioritize a target behavior:
Group of answer choices

Develop an inventory of health behaviors related to the problem

Rate behaviors on their environmental influence

Rate behaviors on importance

Rate behaviors on their changeability
The statement "Rate behaviors on importance" is not one of the steps to prioritize a target behavior in the PRECEDE model.

You are continuing your needs assessment work on cardiovascular health. With your limited budget it is important that any interventions be directed to target only the highest priority environmental factors. Which of the criteria below is NOT related to decisions based on importance?
Group of answer choices

Clearly linked to the health problem.

Can be clearly documented

Has community support to make environmental change

Is prevalent in the population of interest.
The criterion "Is prevalent in the population of interest" is NOT related to decisions based on importance when selecting environmental factors for interventions in the PRECEDE model.

A component of your recommendation is that community HMO’s institute a standard operating policy to issue asthma action plans to physicians for use with each patient who is diagnosed with asthma. This recommendation is provided as part of your assessment of the … ?
Group of answer choices

Interpersonal environment

Organizational environment

Community environment

Societal environment
The recommendation to issue asthma action plans to physicians as part of the assessment is provided within the Organizational environment.

You are developing a PRECEDE model on cardiovascular health. It is important that your limited budget be directed to interventions that target the highest priority behaviors. Which of the following statements is not one of the steps to prioritize a target behavior:
Group of answer choices

Develop an inventory of health behaviors related to the problem

Rate behaviors on their environmental influence

Rate behaviors on importance

Rate behaviors on their changeability
The statement "Rate behaviors on their environmental influence" is not one of the steps to prioritize a target behavior in the PRECEDE model.

You are hired by the health department to conduct a needs assessment of people with diabetes. The PRECEDE model provides a logical series of steps to describe all the factors that influence diabetes in the community. You complete a series of phased assessments. In the behavioral assessment you should examine:
Group of answer choices

environmental links to the behavior, or outcomes of interest.

Predisposing, reinforcing, and enabling factors that influence behaviors

behavioral links to the problems identified in the social or epidemiological analysis

alignment of an intervention to existing policy and administration

In the behavioral assessment of the PRECEDE model for diabetes, you should examine:

Predisposing, reinforcing, and enabling factors that influence behaviors.

You are continuing your needs assessment work on cardiovascular health. With your limited budget it is important that any interventions be directed to target only the highest priority environmental factors. Which of the criteria below is NOT related to decisions based on importance?
Group of answer choices

Clearly linked to the health problem.

Can be clearly documented

Has community support to make environmental change

Is prevalent in the population of interest.
The criterion "Is prevalent in the population of interest" is NOT related to decisions based on importance when selecting environmental factors for interventions in cardiovascular health.
